record.field: SonarQube continuously inspects your code to catch bugs, vulnerabilities and code smells, with quality gates that plug straight into your CI/CD pipeline.

SonarQube continuously inspects your code to catch bugs, vulnerabilities and code smells, with quality gates that plug straight into your CI/CD pipeline.

SonarQube Community Build is the free, self-hosted edition of SonarSource's static analysis platform. It scans your source code across dozens of languages and surfaces bugs, security vulnerabilities and maintainability issues in a single dashboard, then enforces a configurable Quality Gate that can pass or fail every build. Backed by PostgreSQL, it is aimed at development teams that want to automate code-quality and security controls inside their CI/CD workflow and keep full ownership of the analysis data.

Key features
  • Static analysis for 30+ languages (Java, JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, C#, PHP, Go and more), detecting bugs, vulnerabilities and code smells
  • Configurable Quality Gates that pass or fail a build against thresholds, ready to break CI when quality drops
  • Clean as You Code: focus on newly changed code so overall quality improves commit after commit
  • Pull request analysis and CI integration with Jenkins, GitLab, GitHub Actions and Azure DevOps
  • Security rules mapped to OWASP Top 10, CWE and SANS, with remediation guidance for each issue
  • Historical dashboards for test coverage, duplications, complexity and technical debt
Best for

Development teams that want automated code-quality and security gates wired into their CI/CD pipelines. Operational caveats: SonarQube embeds Elasticsearch, so the host must set vm.max_map_count=262144 and raise file-descriptor limits before it will start, allow a couple of GB of RAM, and you should change the default admin/admin credentials on first login.

services: sonarqube: image: sonarqube:community depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:9000' environment: SONAR_JDBC_URL: jdbc:postgresql://db:5432/sonar SONAR_JDBC_USERNAME: sonar SONAR_JDBC_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} volumes: - ./sonarqube_data:/opt/sonarqube/data - ./sonarqube_extensions:/opt/sonarqube/extensions - ./sonarqube_logs:/opt/sonarqube/logs ulimits: nofile: soft: 65536 hard: 65536 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: postgres:17 environment: POSTGRES_DB: sonar POSTGRES_USER: sonar POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U $${POSTGRES_USER} -d $${POSTGRES_DB} interval: 5s timeout: 5s retries: 5 volumes: - ./db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
Password for the bundled PostgreSQL database used by SonarQube.
